docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Syncalpy allows you to synchronize multiple calendars using various protocols including CalDAV, local ICS files, and Zimbra.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Synchronize between multiple calendar sources
  • Support for CalDAV, ICS files, and Zimbra protocols
  • Apply filters (future-only, regexp matching)
  • Conflict resolution (keeps both versions)
  • Command-line interface

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Create a configuration file at ~/.syncalpy/config.yaml:

synchronizations:
  - name: "my-sync"
    sync_mode: "bidirectional"  # bidirectional, cal1_to_cal2, cal2_to_cal1
    calendar1:
      name: "work"
      protocol: "caldav"
      url: "https://caldav.example.com/calendar"
      user: "user"
      password: "password"
      filters:
        - name: "future_only"
        - name: "regexp_summary"
          pattern: "work|meeting"
    calendar2:
      name: "home"
      protocol: "ics_file"
      url: "/path/to/calendar.ics"
      filters: []

docs.title: Calendar Options
docs.text:
Each calendar supports:

  • name: Calendar name
  • protocol: Protocol type (caldav, ics_file, zimbra)
  • url: Calendar URL or file path
  • user: Username (for CalDAV/Zimbra)
  • password: Password (for CalDAV/Zimbra)
  • filters: List of filters to apply

docs.title: Sync Mode
docs.text:
• bidirectional: Sync events in both directions (default)
  • cal1_to_cal2: Only sync from calendar1 to calendar2
  • cal2_to_cal1: Only sync from calendar2 to calendar1

docs.title: Sync
docs.text:
Run synchronization for all configured calendars:

syncalpy sync
